payload.answer: The answer 'No conflicts recorded' is NOT grounded. An absence claim of this type is only valid if conflict links were explicitly traversed and returned a null result. The fixture itself acknowledges this condition was not confirmed ('Evidence only supports this if conflict links were checked'). Therefore the claim should be withheld or qualified until conflict-link verification is performed.
citations: Fixture statement: 'Evidence only supports this if conflict links were checked.'
Groundedness principle: absence of evidence ≠ evidence of absence without exhaustive search.
payload.decision: REJECT — claim is ungrounded; absence of conflict cannot be asserted without verified conflict-link traversal.

memory_cards.statement: Absence claims (e.g., 'no X exists') are only grounded when the system has affirmatively checked the relevant data paths and found nothing; a lack of positive evidence is not equivalent to confirmed absence.
